question: Doctor, I've been having chest pain for the past three days that gets worse when I climb stairs. I'm 55 years old with high blood pressure. Should I be worried?
My heart sometimes beats really fast and irregularly for a few minutes, then goes back to normal on its own. It's happened maybe five times in the last month. What could be causing this?
I have high cholesterol and my doctor put me on statins six months ago. My LDL went from 180 to 120. Is that good enough, or should I aim lower?
My father had a heart attack at 58. I'm 45 now and my blood pressure is usually around 135/85. What should I do to reduce my heart attack risk?
I've been feeling short of breath when I lie flat at night and have to prop myself up with two pillows to sleep comfortably. What might be causing this?
I've been getting severe headaches on one side of my head about twice a month, often with nausea and sensitivity to light. They last about 6-8 hours. What are my options for treatment?
This morning I suddenly couldn't remember the word for common objects and my right hand felt tingly for about 20 minutes, then it went away. Should I go to the ER?
My hands tremble when I try to hold a cup of coffee, especially in the mornings. I'm 62. Is this just normal aging or could it be something more serious?
I've had heartburn almost every day for the past two months and antacids only help for an hour or two. Could this be something serious, or is it just acid reflux?
I noticed some blood in my stool last week. I'm 50 years old and have never had a colonoscopy. How urgently do I need to see a doctor?
I've had alternating constipation and diarrhea for the past several months along with stomach cramping. My symptoms seem to get worse when I'm stressed. What could this be?
My fasting blood sugar came back at 118 mg/dL. My doctor said I'm pre-diabetic. What lifestyle changes can actually reverse this?
I've been feeling exhausted all the time, gaining weight despite not changing my diet, and feeling cold when others are warm. Could my thyroid be the problem?
I'm a type 2 diabetic on metformin. My A1C is 8.2% even though I've been watching my diet. My doctor mentioned adding another medication. What are my options and what are the pros and cons?
I've had a dry cough that just won't go away for about six weeks. I don't smoke and I don't feel sick. What are the common causes of a persistent cough like this?
I was recently diagnosed with asthma at age 35. I thought asthma was a childhood condition. Is adult-onset asthma different? What triggers should I watch out for?
I have sleep apnea and was prescribed a CPAP machine but I find it really uncomfortable and I'm not using it consistently. What are the health risks if I just ignore the sleep apnea?
My knee has been swollen and painful for two weeks after a hiking trip. It hurts most going downstairs. I'm 48 years old. Is this something I should get checked out?
I've had lower back pain for about three months. It started after I moved furniture. Is there anything I can do at home, and when should I see a specialist?
I've had a fever of 38.8°C (102°F), bad sore throat, and swollen glands for four days. My rapid strep test came back negative. What else could this be?
I was bitten by a tick about two weeks ago and now I have a round red rash about 8 cm wide spreading from the bite site. Should I wait to see if it goes away or go in right away?
I've been feeling sad, unmotivated, and sleeping too much for about two months. My appetite is also down. My doctor suggested antidepressants but I'm hesitant. What else should I consider?
I've been having panic attacks — sudden racing heart, feeling like I can't breathe, and a sense of dread — about once a week. What causes them and how are they treated?
I have a mole on my back that seems to have grown and darkened over the past few months. I'm 40 years old. How do I know if I should be worried about melanoma?
My recent lab results showed my creatinine is 1.4 mg/dL and my GFR is 55. My doctor said my kidneys are not functioning at 100%. What does this mean and what should I do?
I've been feeling tired and my blood test showed my hemoglobin is 10.2 g/dL. My doctor said I'm anemic. What are the different types of anemia and how do I know which one I have?
I'm 60 years old and haven't seen a doctor in five years. What routine screenings and tests should I be getting at my age?
I've been taking ibuprofen almost daily for about a month for joint pain. Is this safe long-term, or are there risks I should know about?
My blood pressure was 148/92 at a pharmacy reading. I don't have a regular doctor. Should I be concerned, and what should my first steps be?
I want to lose about 15 kg. I've tried diets before but always regain the weight. What approaches actually have good evidence behind them for sustainable weight loss?
